research_confidence.notes: Architecture and content types well-documented via KA engineering blog and open-source repos (Perseus, Wonder Blocks). Exact accessibility tree roles (AXButton vs AXLink) for specific buttons are inferred from standard React/WCAG patterns since direct macOS accessibility tree dumps were not available. Button text ('Check', 'Skip', 'Next question') confirmed from KA support articles and community posts. Timing values are conservative estimates based on SPA behavior with GraphQL backend and Fastly CDN. Perseus widget types confirmed from GitHub source (30+ widget dirs). Khanmigo AI tutor may introduce unexpected modals on paid/district accounts.

timing.notes: React SPA; in-set question transitions ~1.5s (Perseus in-place); initial loads slower; YouTube iframe adds latency.

never_click.reason: Permanently deletes user account and all progress data
Ends the session; re-authentication required to continue
Alternative text for logout; ends session
Teacher-facing action that removes a student from a class
Removes the learner from an enrolled class
Opens a report dialog for content issues; does not advance learning and may flag account for unusual activity
Navigates away from Khan Academy to YouTube; video progress will NOT be tracked and no credit is awarded
Opens donation page; not related to coursework
Opens account settings; not related to coursework and could change configuration
Course-wide navigation arrow (~23x25px AXButton at top of page)
Course-wide navigation arrow that skips backward past completed content
accessibility skip-link, a no-op for navigation — observed wasted cycle 2026-06-11 (LLM picked it as the nav answer)
accessibility skip-link, a no-op for navigation — observed wasted cycle 2026-06-11 (LLM picked it as the nav answer)
on summary cards this re-does a COMPLETED set; forward is the Up next link (completion indicator)

platform_quirks.description: Khanmigo chat overlay (paid/district accounts) can obscure content; dismiss via its X (top-right) before interacting. Icon bottom-right.
After a practice set with exactly 1 wrong, a 'Bonus question' may appear; it MUST be answered or skipped or the exercise is NOT marked complete. Correct answer upgrades to Proficient.
'Skip' marks the question INCORRECT (and shows the walkthrough). Never click unless terminally stuck. Grays out after a correct answer.
Videos must play to the very end (every second; <=2x speed OK; teacher-assigned: >=90% at <=2x). Closing early marks incomplete.
Under load/YouTube-blocked, a fallback player may serve (no speed controls); 'Switch to full player' link bottom-right returns to YouTube embed.
Mastery Challenges (math only): 6 questions / 3 skills, one per 12h window, appear on Course page only when 3+ Familiar and 1+ Proficient.
Expression inputs: explicit parentheses ('1/(2p)' not '1/2p'), brackets entered as parentheses, strict PEMDAS — formatting errors mark correct answers wrong.
First-visit 'Welcome to Khan Academy!' modal (Learner/Teacher/Parent) must be dismissed before content is accessible.
Left lesson sidebar collapse state persists; if links are missing from the tree, the sidebar may be collapsed — expand via its arrow button.
Quizzes/unit tests are sequential: no going back, no skipping ahead; wrong answers can level skills DOWN.
One exercise set mixes Perseus widget types per question; classify each question from its CURRENT widgets (see _UNKNOWN_GUIDE.md).
Buttons whose AX name/description says 'opens a modal' spawn portal dialogs at shallow depth outside the AXWebArea; wait portal_render before interacting.
ANSWER-AREA widgets only: enumerate collapsed/hidden answer content (menus etc.) during extraction; chrome popups are exempt (mis-scoping this caused dropdown builds on a checkbox question).

Cmd+R (page reload) CLEARS staged answers on the current question (per Jesse 2026-06-11 — legitimate clearing mechanism). NEVER reload between clicking Check and confirming the submission registered: a reload in that window can eat the submission (suspected cause of the Q4 'X' on the 2026-06-11 unit test — the stale BT pressed Cmd+R 3s after Check).

SCREEN SHARING EATS SYNTHETIC EVENTS (root-caused 2026-06-11, probe-proven both ways): when the Screen Sharing app holds macOS foreground on the Mac, CGEvent clicks/drags are consumed and never reach Chrome — BTs report success, nothing stages. When Screen Sharing is not foreground, identical events work. If actions repeatedly report success with no staged effect, suspect the foreground app FIRST. Mac-side pre-BT foreground guard filed to claude-0.

WRONG-ANSWER RESET MECHANISM (KHAN): browser refresh (press_key 'r' + cmd) is the reliable reset on wrong-answer states — it reloads the question fresh. Khan's 'Start over' button is CONFIRMED UNRELIABLE (2026-05-19: did not restore initial state). 'Try again' resets the widget in-place on practice exercises and is acceptable for drag/tile widgets where a refresh would lose scroll position; prefer Cmd+R when the form state is suspect. NEVER refresh between Check and verifying the submission registered (refresh in that window can eat the submission).

completion_model.video: Must watch every second of the video (no skipping). Playback up to 2x is fine. Completion shown as full colored bar with checkmark icon in sidebar. For assignments: 90% watched at ≤2x speed.
completion_model.article: Auto-completed when visited and scrolled through. No explicit 'Mark as complete' button. Simply opening and scrolling to the bottom registers as read. Green checkmark appears in sidebar.
completion_model.exercise: Must answer all questions in the set (typically 4-7 questions). >70% correct = 'Practiced' level. All correct = 'Proficient'. With bonus question: 1 wrong + bonus correct = 'Proficient'. Exercise NOT complete until all questions (including bonus if offered) are answered or skipped.
completion_model.quiz: Must answer all questions. Scored as percentage. Results affect mastery levels for skills covered. No going back to previous questions.
completion_model.unit_test: Must answer all questions. Results can level skills up or down. Covers all skills in the unit. No going back.
completion_model.course_challenge: 30 questions covering entire course. Results affect mastery levels across all course skills. No going back.
completion_model.mastery_challenge: 6 questions covering 3 skills (2 each). Math courses only. 12-hour window. 2 correct on a skill = level up, 2 wrong = level down, 1 each = no change.

bot_detection.known_measures: Khan Academy uses Fastly CDN which provides basic bot detection and rate limiting. No CAPTCHA documented for normal browsing. The platform tracks video watching behavior closely (scrub detection, play state monitoring via YouTube API) to prevent credit gaming. Google Cloud App Engine has default DDoS protections. Static assets served from kastatic.org and kasandbox.org domains. No explicit Cloudflare or dedicated bot management layer documented in public sources.
bot_detection.recommended_delays: Add 1-2s random delay between actions. For exercises, wait at least 2s after Check before clicking Next. For video pages, do not interact with the player programmatically faster than a human would. Avoid submitting answers faster than 5s per question to prevent pattern detection. Between page navigations, wait 2-3s minimum. For rapid sequential exercises, vary timing between 3-8s per question.

screen_type_definitions: screen_types/*.yaml (one per type; supervisor-owned; THE program per type)
